metrics.narrative: Customer_Business_Problem__c lists a broad efficiency/savings agenda (faster sourcing cycles, more event capacity, AI-driven supplier scouting/risk, autonomous negotiation, supplier onboarding), backed by a real forcing function in Compelling_Event__c: a 'CEO mandate to reduce cost and increase efficiencies.' Zycus quoted early-adopter benchmarks on the 21 Oct 2025 call (4-6% tail-spend savings over 4-5 months; 70-80% reduction in negotiation time), but McAfee has not validated these against its own data. Instead, the live POC has surfaced a different, unquantified metric risk: supplier response rates across the five use-case events have been very low (e.g., 1 of 5 suppliers responded on the laptop RFQ; 0 of 6 on the financial-data-platform RFI, per the May 2026 event trackers), which Aitor called a possible 'structural limitation' (24 Jun 2026 email) rather than the promised efficiency gain.

champion.narrative: Aitor Gonzalez Martin has driven the deal since the 5 May 2025 intro call, personally coordinating the POC agreement, all 5 use-case events, internal stakeholder management (Alejandro Lanzavecchia, Puri Alende, a McAfee purchasing specialist), and the Nippon Gas reference call. His advocacy is real (detailed feedback, willingness to relaunch failed test events with simulated suppliers, direct email access to the relevant stakeholder of Sales), but his tone has shifted from curious to skeptical: on 2026-06-24 he said the underwhelming reference call 'reinforced the impression that this capability is still relatively new for Zycus,' and flagged persistent supplier non-response as a possible 'structural limitation.' He remains cooperative (1 Jul 2026 reply committing to a week-of-24-Jul completion target) but is not yet evidenced as advocating internally to the CFO or CEO.

competition.narrative: Competitors__c and related fields are blank in Salesforce, but per AIS_Why__c, McAfee ran a formal down-select from 9 vendors to a 3-vendor shortlist specifically for this AI/ANA evaluation, and on 17 Oct 2025 Aitor stated McAfee was running the POC against 'one or two vendors' in parallel, an identity never disclosed to the relevant stakeholder (an orchestration tool named at the 5 May 2025 intro call) and PACTEM (a post-sourcing contract-negotiation tool Mazen flagged 17 Oct 2025) have not resurfaced in 8+ months and are not confirmed head-to-head ANA rivals. The real competitive exposure is the unnamed 1-2 parallel POC finalist(s) whose positioning and pricing remain invisible to Zycus.

identify_pain.narrative: Customer_Business_Problem__c and the live email threads confirm specific, real pain: slow sourcing cycles, capacity constraints on running more sourcing events, weak vendor/supplier risk assessment, and a stated desire for AI-driven supplier scouting and autonomous negotiation. The pain is validated by a genuine forcing function, Compelling_Event__c: 'CEO mandate to reduce cost and increase efficiencies.' The pain owner is Aitor Gonzalez Martin, who has run the day-to-day POC and articulated both the opportunity and its current gaps (e.g., supplier non-response) directly to Zycus.

paper_process.narrative: The POC agreement itself was fully executed: Next_Step_History__c shows the agreement retrieved and signed by a McAfee signatory in April 2026, kickoff on 2026-04-23, and the POC officially launched 2026-05-05. However, no MSA, Order Form, or SOW discussion has started for a post-POC commercial phase, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank, so there is no renewal-driven forcing date. Drafting the post-POC contracting plan now would prevent paper from becoming the next multi-week delay after use-case sign-off.

economic_buyer.narrative: X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c are all blank/false, and no OpportunityContactRole names a CFO or budget owner. Next_Step_History__c shows McAfee held its own internal CFO meeting on 2026-06-11 and a CEO/CIO video review the week of 2026-06-24 to review the ANA highlight video, but Zycus was not on either call and has not seen the outcome. Aitor's own stated gate ('if the product is more expensive than the savings obtained, we're not going to proceed,' 17 Oct 2025) makes him the closest current proxy for EB screening, but he sits on the procurement/analytics side, not the final budget holder.

decision_process.narrative: What_is_the_decision_process__c is blank in Salesforce, but the call/email sequence reveals the real process: a formal down-select from 9 vendors to 3 finalists (SF AIS_Why__c), a live POC with 5 use-case events run jointly by McAfee procurement (Aitor Gonzalez Martin, Alejandro Lanzavecchia, Puri Alende, and a McAfee purchasing specialist) and Zycus delivery, weekly cadence calls, and internal executive reviews (CFO meeting 11 Jun 2026, CEO/CIO video review week of 24 Jun 2026) Zycus has not been invited into. The next gate is end-to-end validation of all 5 use cases, targeted for the week of 2026-07-24 per Aitor's 1 Jul 2026 email, after which McAfee has indicated it will move to a decision.

decision_criteria.narrative: Business_Requirements__c is blank, but Customer_Business_Problem__c defines a detailed evaluation checklist (savings, cycle time, event capacity, vendor assessment, AI-driven supplier scouting/risk, autonomous negotiation, supplier onboarding). Per AIS_Why__c, McAfee tightened its shortlist from 9 vendors to 3 specifically to evaluate AI/ANA capability, and the live POC is now testing these criteria concretely across five priced use cases (QA testing services ~$30k, social-media RFI ~$84k, financial-data platform ~$30k, laptop RFQ ~$18k, data-modeling software ~$40k), each with a named McAfee-side owner tracking supplier response and completion as the pass/fail bar.

bullets.full: Close date 2026-07-31 (24 days out) is not credible. The POC is still in progress (2 use-case events active, deadline extended per 19 Jun; 29 Jun check-in on 'outstanding use cases and overall timeline'), and no commercial, pricing, or contract step has been logged. Even if the POC concludes positively in the next 1-2 weeks, the remaining gates (EB validation, pricing negotiation, MSA/Order Form, InfoSec if required, signature) cannot complete in ~2 weeks. The date will slip to late August or September at the earliest.
bullets.text: Close date 31 Jul 2026 (24 days out) is not credible.

bullets.full: Unknown POC competitor creates invisible political risk. Zycus does not know if the competitor has a stronger internal advocate (e.g., an executive sponsor Zycus has not met), a preferred-vendor relationship, or a lower price that has already been validated. If the competitor has been more aggressive in mapping and engaging the economic buyer or senior stakeholders, Zycus could lose the POC even with equal or better technical results.

items.detail: Aitor set a strict ROI gate: 'we're not going to invest into something that has no ROI. If the product is more expensive than the savings obtained, we're not going to proceed' (17 Oct). Zycus has not sent a commercial proposal or validated pricing against McAfee's budget. If the unknown POC competitor has lower pricing or Zycus's spend-based tier pricing is too high, McAfee could choose the cheaper option even if Zycus's POC results are stronger.

No economic buyer (CFO or budget owner) is mapped or engaged. The 11 Jun Next_Step mentions a 'McAfee meeting w CFO on Monday' with no follow-up, and the 4 Jun note mentions a CEO/CIO review of the ANA video, but this is a passive internal review, not active Zycus engagement with the EB. With 24 days to close and no EB sign-off on the $200k investment, the deal cannot move to the relevant stakeholder or Negotiation. The champion (Aitor) can drive the POC to a positive outcome, but he cannot close the deal.

Close date 2026-07-31 (24 days out) is not credible. The POC is still in progress (2 use-case events active, deadline extended per 19 Jun; 29 Jun check-in on 'outstanding use cases and overall timeline'), and no commercial, pricing, or contract step has been logged. Even if the POC concludes positively in the next 1-2 weeks, the remaining gates (EB validation, pricing negotiation, MSA/Order Form, InfoSec if required, signature) cannot complete in ~2 weeks. The date will slip to late August or September at the earliest.

items.detail: Unknown POC competitor creates invisible political risk. Zycus does not know if the competitor has a stronger internal advocate (e.g., an executive sponsor Zycus has not met), a preferred-vendor relationship, or a lower price that has already been validated. If the competitor has been more aggressive in mapping and engaging the economic buyer or senior stakeholders, Zycus could lose the POC even with equal or better technical results.

McAfee's majority spend is indirect services (marketing, facilities, software renewals), and the 21 Oct demo showed that complex/intangible services may require human-in-loop for non-pricing negotiations (e.g., SEO scope, hypercare weeks, contract terms). If too many of McAfee's service categories require human intervention, the autonomous-negotiation value proposition shrinks — fewer deals run fully autonomously means lower throughput gains and weaker savings, undermining the ROI justification. The buyer has not yet confirmed how many of their tail-spend categories are quotable (fully autonomous) vs. complex (human-in-loop required).
items.status: acknowledged (Zycus positioned configurable human handoff as solution)

items.detail: Persistent platform reliability issues during the POC: event-closing bugs, deadline-extension confusion, notification misrouting (Coral/iSource branding confusion), missing internal-expert-response feature, and contact-management gaps across multiple use-case events.

Low supplier response rates across all 5 POC use-case events (e.g., 1 of 5 laptop suppliers responded; 0 of 6 financial-data-platform suppliers responded), which the champion called a possible 'structural limitation.'
Nippon Gas reference call (18 Jun 2026) underwhelmed: older ANA version, only ~20 events created, reinforcing doubt about product maturity.
No commercial/pricing proposal delivered despite being committed since 21 Oct 2025; Aitor's explicit ROI gate remains untested against real numbers.
CFO/economic buyer never engaged directly by Zycus; only internal McAfee reviews (CFO meeting 11 Jun, CEO/CIO video review week of 24 Jun) with no Zycus visibility into the outcome.

Close date of 31 Jul 2026 sits only about a week after McAfee's own week-of-24-Jul target to finish POC testing, with zero days budgeted for commercial/contract steps; the date has already slipped roughly 8 times since deal creation.

reasons.detail: This $200K Best Case (forecasted) opportunity had its close date pushed from Jun 30 to Jul 31 on 29 June, and the ANA POC still shows 0 of 5 use cases completed with supplier-responsiveness flagged as a structural gap. No economic buyer is mapped (only Nick Priest, Head of Global Procurement, is engaged; the CFO is unnamed), so there is no confirmed budget sponsor behind the slip. If the quarter's forecast leans on McAfee, a stalled POC plus an unproven CFO means the deal likely pushes again and the status-quo Workday/Omnia stack wins by default.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-05-06 (~14 months in pipeline). Entered the current Shortlisted stage on 2026-03-03 (per Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c) - about 126 days (~18 weeks) ago, unusually long dwell time for this stage. The close date has been revised roughly eight times since creation (2025-12-05 -> 2026-12-04 -> 2026-09-18 -> 2026-01-30 -> 2026-02-27 -> 2026-04-30 -> 2026-05-29 -> 2026-04-17 -> 2026-05-29 -> 2026-06-04 -> 2026-06-30 -> 2026-07-31, per OpportunityFieldHistory), most recently on 2026-06-29. With today 2026-07-07 and close_date 2026-07-31, only 24 days remain, but McAfee's own POC-completion target is the week of 2026-07-24 (per Aitor's 1 Jul 2026 email) - leaving roughly one week to clear pricing, EB sign-off, and paper after use-case validation even lands on time.
evidence: 0 of 5 POC use-case events completed end-to-end as of 24 Jun 2026 (Aitor email)
No commercial proposal delivered despite a 2025-10-21 Zycus commitment (Next_Step_History__c)
Nippon Gas reference call (18 Jun 2026) 'reinforced the impression that this capability is still relatively new for Zycus' (Aitor, 24 Jun 2026 email)
north_star_verdict.headline: Aitor remains an engaged, cooperative champion, but the core POC deliverable has stalled: zero of five use-case events have been completed end-to-end, the champion has floated a 'structural limitation' on supplier response, the Nippon Gas reference call reinforced doubt rather than removing it, and no commercial proposal has ever reached McAfee, with the buyer's own retest target (week of 24 Jul 2026) leaving only about a week before the 31 Jul close date.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Move off Best Case to Pipeline; realistic close is Q3 2026 (Sept) pending POC completion, a delivered commercial proposal, and direct CFO/EB engagement.

flags: The core value prop (supplier response rate) is under live stress-test: across the 5 POC events only a fraction of suppliers have responded (e.g., 1 of 5 on the laptop RFQ), and Aitor has floated a 'structural limitation' - winning back confidence here before week-of-24-Jul is the single highest-leverage lever.

No commercial proposal has reached McAfee 8+ months after Zycus committed to one; with pricing untested against Aitor's explicit ROI gate and only ~24 days to the current close date, get pricing in front of him now rather than after POC sign-off.
best_practice_check.summary: Three deal-aware levers stand out for the next 2-4 weeks.

competitive_position.summary: McAfee ran a 9-vendor field down to a 3-vendor ANA shortlist (SF AIS_Why__c) and Zycus remains one of the finalists in an active, live POC, but the identity of the other 1-2 finalists has never been disclosed (Aitor, 17 Oct 2025: 'one or two vendors'). Two adjacent tools surfaced early in the relationship, Zip (an orchestration tool, 5 May 2025) and PACTEM (a post-sourcing contract-negotiation tool, 17 Oct 2025), have not resurfaced in over 8 months and are not confirmed head-to-head ANA competitors. The single strongest current threat is the unnamed parallel POC finalist(s), because their positioning and pricing are completely invisible to Zycus while the buyer's own decision window (week of 24 Jul 2026) approaches.

competitors.name: Zip (orchestration tool)
competitors.quote: Mazen (5 May 2025 intro call): 'we're looking at things in the market like Zip, like Zycus.'
competitors.how_we_win: Zip was mentioned early as an orchestration tool McAfee was exploring, but it has not resurfaced in the POC or recent discussions — likely faded as McAfee narrowed to autonomous negotiation (ANA) rather than full orchestration replacement (they kept Omnia).

competitors.name: PACTEM (post-sourcing contract negotiation)
competitors.quote: Mazen (17 Oct discovery call): 'I don't know if you're familiar with PACTEM... some of the work they do is more post-sourcing, but still negotiation on the contract side.'
competitors.how_we_win: PACTEM is adjacent (post-award contract negotiation) rather than competitive to ANA (pre-award sourcing negotiation); Mazen mentioned it as a reference point, not an active alternative — not a current threat unless McAfee decides to split the workflow.

competitors.name: Unidentified parallel POC finalist(s) (McAfee's 3-vendor shortlist)
competitors.quote: I'm going to be fully transparent, one or two vendors.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 17 Oct 2025; SF AIS_Why__c
competitors.how_we_win: Ask Aitor directly, now that the shortlist is confirmed at 3, to name the other two finalists so Zycus can address gaps head-on before the week-of-24-Jul decision window.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: McAfee mentioned looking at orchestration tools like Zip alongside Zycus during early discovery.
competitors.how_we_win: Position ANA's autonomous negotiation depth against Zip's orchestration-only scope; no re-mention in 14 months suggests it is no longer active competition.

competitors.name: PACTEM
competitors.quote: Mazen raised Pactem as an interest in post-sourcing, contract-side negotiation, distinct from Zycus's pre-award autonomous negotiation.
competitors.how_we_win: Reframe Pactem as complementary, not competitive: ANA covers pre-award negotiation, Pactem covers post-sourcing contract admin; no overlap to defend.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: McAfee ANA is a $200k Shortlisted-stage deal in an active, buyer-driven proof-of-concept with an engaged but unaided champion. It lacks any confirmed economic-buyer engagement, has an undelivered commercial proposal 9 months after it was promised, and faces an unnamed parallel POC competitor - all while carrying a close date only 22 days out.

deal_risk.text: The 31 Jul 2026 close date (22 days from today) assumes POC completion, EB sign-off, commercial negotiation, and paper process all finish in three weeks with zero commercial conversation yet started - very unlikely to hold.

Persistent platform reliability issues during the live POC (event-closing bugs, notification misrouting, missing internal-expert-response feature) risk souring the champion's confidence in product maturity right when a decision is approaching.

win_position.text: Aitor Gonzalez Martin has championed the deal since 5 May 2025 (about 14 months ago) and personally arranged a customer reference call, but no economic buyer (CFO/CEO/CIO) has ever been directly engaged by Zycus - the 11 Jun 2026 and 4 Jun 2026 notes describe only passive, McAfee-internal reviews.
McAfee is evaluating Zycus in a formal one-or-two-vendor POC bake-off (Aitor, 17 Oct 2025, about 9 months ago), and the competitor's identity and standing remain unknown to Zycus.
The 18 Jun 2026 (about 3 weeks ago) Nippon Gas reference call reportedly underwhelmed McAfee, citing an older ANA version and only about 20 events created, reinforcing doubt about product maturity.

Pain is clearly and repeatedly articulated: McAfee wants to stop 'negotiating for 3 weeks to save $22,000' on tactical spend (Mazen El-Haidari, 21 Oct 2025, about 9 months ago) and free strategic buyers for higher-value work.

deal_momentum.text: The POC is live and buyer-driven: Aitor kicked off 2 additional use cases around 11 Jun 2026 (about 4 weeks ago) and is actively working through 5 total use-case events as of the 29 Jun 2026 (about 10 days ago) Next Step.
Supplier response rates are very low across POC events (e.g. 1 of 5 laptop suppliers, 0 of 6 financial-data-platform suppliers responded), which the champion called a possible structural limitation.
No commercial or pricing proposal has been delivered despite being committed by Zycus on the 21 Oct 2025 call (about 9 months ago, still unresolved).

customer_commitment.text: Aitor personally introduced McAfee's contact to a reference customer (Nippon Gas) and joined the debrief around 15-18 Jun 2026 (about 3 weeks ago), showing active personal investment in validating the solution.

The only economic-buyer touchpoints on record are internal McAfee-only reviews (CFO meeting 11 Jun 2026, CEO/CIO video review week of 24 Jun 2026) that Zycus was not part of and has not seen the outcome of.

hard.ais_why:
• The products on this deal are iSaaS, Agentic AI, and ANA, and Zycus has flagged AI as Very Critical for the account. The 5/4/2026 next-step note records the McAfee ANA POC launched on 5/5. On the 5/5/2025 Avoma call, the buyer confirmed direct interest in Zycus's autonomous negotiation and spend dynamics capabilities.
• The 1/19/2026 rep note captures McAfee wanting to see whether the vendor can deliver on its Agentic AI innovation claims. The 10/21/2025 note documents Aitor pushing on integrating Zycus's autonomous negotiation with Omnia for indirect tail-spend orchestration, with detailed back-and-forth on AI accuracy, hallucination prevention, benchmark confidence, and human-in-the-loop design.
• The buyer's own POC scope requires an evidence-based business case validated through real-world use cases, plus scalability and operational viability post-POC across categories. McAfee tightened the shortlist from 9 vendors to 3 explicitly for the AI and ANA evaluation, which is enterprise-grade commitment, not curiosity.
